text: Drugmakers ask Supreme Court to restore abortion pill access by mail - The Washington Post
fullText: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the 5th Circuit ruled that the commonly used abortion drug mifepristone can only be picked up in-person.

text: Trump disapproval reaches new high, Post-ABC-Ipsos poll finds - The Washington Post
fullText: Democrats now hold a five-point advantage in support for Congress, up from two points in February.

text: Boxing results: Benavidez stops Ramirez, becomes three-division champ - ESPN
fullText: David Benavidez dominated Gilberto Ramirez to win the WBA and WBO cruiserweight titles on Saturday.

text: Olivia Rodrigo Recalls Working With “Acting Legend” Jake Paul, Sings “Drivers License” Parody on ‘SNL’ - The Hollywood Reporter
fullText: The pop star made a callback to Paul's 2024 boxing match against Mike Tyson, joking that they used to discuss their futures and he said, "one day I really wanna beat up old guys on Netflix."

text: Trump says he is reviewing Iran's new offer but doubts it is 'acceptable' - NBC News
fullText: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he was reviewing a new offer from the Iranian regime to end the war, but offered little optimism that the proposal would succeed."I will soon be reviewing the plan that Iran has just sent to us, but can’t imagine that…

text: Washington mother's final moments captured on horrific 911 call: "He's got a gun!" - CBS News
fullText: Gloria Choi and her friends called Lakewood, Washington, 911 four times in 48 hours to report her being stalked by an ex-boyfriend. Two days later, he ran her off the road and riddled her truck with bullets as she was on the line with a 911 dispatcher.

text: Joel Embiid’s big night, Boston’s missed 3s help 76ers complete 3-1 comeback, take series - NBC Sports
fullText: Philadelphia beat Boston in a series for the first time since 1982, and it was the first time the 76ers ever came from 3-1 down to win.

text: Kim Kardashian Skips Early Part of Miami Grand Prix, Attends North West's L.A. Pop-Up - Yahoo
fullText: Kim Kardashian didn't make it to the first night of her daughter North West's pop-up event in Los Angeles ... but, don't think she doesn't care -- she's hitting night #2 instead.

text: Germany says U.S. troop withdrawal ‘anticipated', Spain and Italy could be next - NPR
fullText: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.

text: Trump says new strikes possible if Iran "misbehaves" - Axios
fullText: Trump is seriously considering ordering new military action against Iran to try and break the current stalemate.

text: Canes Announce Second Round Schedule - NHL.com
fullText: Carolina faces Philadelphia in the second round of 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs

text: Golden Tempo takes the Kentucky Derby as Cherie DeVaux becomes the 1st woman to train its winner - NPR
fullText: Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.

text: It's Done! SAG-AFTRA & Studios Reach New (& Bigger) Deal [Exclusive] - Deadline
fullText: SAG-AFTRA and the AMPTP today have reached an agreement on a new contract. No one has made it official yet, but expect news later today or Sunday.

text: Warren Buffett says 'we've never had people in a more gambling mood than now' - Fortune
fullText: "But that doesn't mean that investing is terrible. It does mean that prices for an awful lot of things will look very silly."

text: The new surgeon general nominee has a MAHA problem - Politico

Taiwan's Lai lands in Eswatini in a trip delayed by lack of overflight clearance - NPR

text: Greg Abel’s message to Berkshire shareholders, post-Buffett: be patient - Financial Times

text: Box Office: ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Struts to $32.5 Million on Opening Day - Variety
